Measurement of the subthermal neutron induced fission
GELINA, operated at 40 Hz repetition rate with
2 micro-sec burst width and average electron current
back with an evaporated 25 micro-g/cm2 layer of 6-LiF
in the center of a large vacuum chamber. The fissile
deposit was prepared by suspension spraying of Pu-
acetate, the 241-Pu solution being freshly separated
from the 241-Am ingrowth. The isotopic composition was
two separate runs were performed with the neutron beam
incident on both a Li-Pu and Pu-Li sandwich.
Two 30 cm2 large surface barrier detectors
placed outside the neutron beam in a low geometry.
Data obtained from author on tape.
